title: ANM Apo/Holo Flexibility Comparison — AOH1996 Cryptic Pocket
method: Anisotropic Network Model (ANM), Atilgan et al. 2001
chain_note: AOH ground truth uses chains A+B only (ZQZ ligand in 8GLA is on chains A and B; chain C carries no ligand). Apo 1W60 also has chains A+B only. Both structures use 48 AOH residues for a like-for-like comparison.
interpretation: Apo PCNA (1W60): pocket residues are 14% LESS flexible than background (fold-change 0.857), consistent with a rigidly packed region. Holo PCNA (8GLA): pocket residues are 16% MORE flexible than background (fold-change 1.157). Delta fold-change = +0.300, consistent with a ligand-associated flexibility increase at this site. ANM is a physics-based approximation on static coordinates; it does not prove apo-to-holo pocket opening or confirm cryptic-pocket status. MD simulation is required for that conclusion.
scientific_note: The apo->holo flexibility difference is consistent with structural signatures sometimes seen at cryptic sites, but does not confirm the AOH1996 site is a genuine cryptic pocket. ANM does not simulate ligand-induced dynamics. This result is hypothesis-supporting, not confirmatory.
